basics.name: Andrea W Jans
basics.summary: Software engineer and IT leader with 30+ years of experience building and optimizing systems across industries. Combines deep technical expertise in full-stack development, databases, and infrastructure with a proven track record of leading teams and delivering measurable business impact — including $200K+ in annual savings at American Electric Company. Completed a B.S. in Computer Science at UH Mānoa, with published research in AI-assisted software effort estimation and an NSF fellowship in data engineering.
highlights: Led CMMC and NIST compliance training at American Electric Company, reducing security risk exposure across 4 locations and 190 users.
Published peer-reviewed research on AI-assisted software effort estimation in UH Mānoa's undergraduate research journal.
Delivered $310K+ in cost savings at American Electric Company through cloud migration, in-house IT transition, and licensing optimization.
Reduced deployment time by 90% at Viewpoint Construction Software by building custom tooling to capture and replay customizations.

work.summary: Supported faculty research in AI and software engineering while serving as grader for BUS 619. Applied Python and LLM-based toolchains to test research hypotheses.
highlights: Built Python programs leveraging LLM and RAG toolchains to empirically test research hypotheses in software engineering estimation.
Evaluated student work across homework, quizzes, and exams, providing detailed feedback to support learning outcomes.

work.summary: Led weekly ICS 101 lab sections, developed original instructional materials, and supported student learning through office hours and grading.
highlights: Designed and delivered lab curriculum for ICS 101, translating course concepts into hands-on exercises for introductory CS students.
Provided individualized and group support during office hours, helping students with concepts and assignments.

work.summary: Delivered end-user technical support and server administration through effective communication, relationship-building, and problem-solving skills. Led IT department with up to 3 direct reports managing activities for 190 users. Controlled $6M budget, created and managed processes, and maintained compliance efforts. Executed identity and access management, IT strategy, and customer service functions for teams across 4 locations.
highlights: Cut capital expenditures by $100K through cloud server migration, reduced licensing costs by $10K annually by adopting Microsoft 365, and lowered monthly telecom expenses by $500.
Drove $200K in annual savings by transitioning third-party technical support provider to in-house function.

Strengthened cybersecurity and IT processes with new security controls, including VPN tunnels, new password policies to control password requirements, and technologies to improve email and cloud storage reliability.

work.supervisor: Aaron Sahlstrom; Director, Client Services Operations
work.summary: Conceptualized innovative, custom solutions after engaging clients to understand pain points, business needs, and technical requirements. Leveraged practical communication skills to understand needs, deploy customizations, and guide customers through implementation. Recommended budget management and continuous improvement projects to boost productivity and cost savings.
highlights: Reduced time to deploy by 90% by writing tools to capture customizations.
Developed materials and trained specialists in tools to ensure consistency and data integrity.
Improved customer satisfaction by 80% by implementing new processes to improve customer service and support.

highlights: Reduced time to pick and put by a factor of 10 by improving performance of SQL queries.

work.summary: Maintained Extract-Transform-Load (ETL) processes and critical business reports.
highlights: Created a library of C# Attribute Classes to automate reading from a database into objects.

work.summary: Created macros to extract, transform, and load data using MS Excel.
highlights: Improved performance of Visual Basic for Applications (VBA) macros.
Tuned SQL queries to further enhance performance.

work.summary: Optimized backend code to enhance performance.
highlights: Reduced time to load email by 80% by caching static data and improving query performance.

work.summary: Brought in to research, document, and solve performance issues with Extract-Transform-Load (ETL) processes and critical business reports.
highlights: Reduced time to extract, transform, and load data from overnight to under an hour.
Decreased time to generate reports by 90% by optimizing SQL queries.

work.summary: Created software to automate business processes.
highlights: Created COM plugin for FoxPro to process credit cards over encrypted internet connection.
Managed selection of Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) system to replace legacy system.

work.summary: Ensured the successful delivery of client projects. Administered corporate network and servers, maintaining a secure and efficient IT infrastructure. Supported developers with innovative problem-solving, streamlining workflows, and implementing continuous integration.
highlights: Mentored and guided a team of 12 software engineers, fostering professional growth and technical excellence.
Proactively identified and addressed project challenges, ensuring smooth execution and client satisfaction.
Used continuous integration and unit/system testing to streamline build process and deliver the project early.

work.summary: Designed and developed a smart-client application to enable representatives to track gifts to schools and professors, and associate positive outcomes to justify activities.
Completed the next phase of features for the Knowledge Management System started while working for Luminant Worldwide.

Managed client-driven software projects from post-sale handoff through delivery, leading a team of 6 engineers. Served as primary client liaison, overseeing planning, task assignments, and resource allocation for complex web-based solutions.
highlights: Mentored and guided a team of 6 software engineers, fostering professional growth and technical excellence.

highlights: Mentored and guided a team of 10 software engineers, fostering professional growth and technical excellence.

publications.title: Artificial Estimators: A Study in Training Systems for Expert Estimating Software Development Task Efforts
publications.journal: Horizons: The UH Mānoa Undergraduate Research Journal
publications.summary: Effort estimation in software engineering has long been a persistent challenge, with traditional expert-based and algorithmic approaches often producing inaccurate and inconsistent results. This research investigates whether large language models (LLMs), specifically when combined with Generative Pretrained Transformers (GPT) and Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), can provide reliable estimates of the effort required for software development tasks. A mixed-methods research design was used: historical project data was collected, standardized, and used to fine-tune an open-source LLM, which was then tested against untrained configurations, with and without RAG. Thirty feature requests were used as test cases in multiple runs to evaluate performance. The results indicate that while LLMs can generate effort estimates, their outputs are highly inconsistent. The research suggests that, despite their potential, current LLM-based systems cannot yet replace expert judgment in estimating software projects. Instead, they highlight the need for further refinement, the integration of domain-specific training, and standardized data practices to harness LLMs as supportive—rather than standalone—tools for estimating software engineering efforts.
